Title: Database schema, encryption, get_api_key, and endpoint wiring
Description:
**Scope:** Add encrypted API key column to candidate table, build encryption helpers, add get_api_key core function, and wire save into existing PUT endpoint.

- ALTER candidate table: add `candidate_api_key` TEXT column
- Idempotent migration in `_ensure_candidate_schema()` in src/data/database.py
- Add `cryptography` (Fernet) to requirements
- Encryption key from env var (e.g. `ASTRAL_ENCRYPTION_KEY`)
- Two helper functions in database.py: `_encrypt_value(plaintext) -> ciphertext`, `_decrypt_value(ciphertext) -> plaintext`
- `save_candidate()` encrypts `candidate_api_key` before writing
- `get_candidate()` returns `candidate_api_key` (encrypted) as part of the candidate dict — the raft carries it
- Add `get_api_key(candidate: dict) -> str` to src/core/candidate.py: decrypts `candidate_api_key` from the candidate dict, raises `ValueError` if missing or blank — no fallback to system key
- Extend existing `PUT /api/candidates/<id>/data` in ui/api/candidate.py: pop `api_key` from body (same pattern as `state` override), encrypt, pass to `save_candidate()` as `candidate_api_key` — no new endpoint
- Never echo the key value back in any response; list endpoint returns truthiness only (see list view subissue)
- Update database.py module docstring per ASTRAL_CODE_RULES 1.1

**Layer:** src/data/database.py, src/core/candidate.py, ui/api/candidate.py

Title: CONFIG_TASKS refactor and TOKEN_SOURCES in config.py
Description:
**Scope:** Replace AGENT_CONFIG with CONFIG_TASKS and add config-driven token resolution mapping.

- Replace `AGENT_CONFIG` with `CONFIG_TASKS` — task key is the index, each entry keeps only code-owned fields:
  - `response_schema` (dict), `vectors` (list), `grading_mode` (string), `requires_candidate_key` (bool), `trigger_state` (string)
- Prompt content fields (system_prompt, task_prompt, cached_blocks, uncached_blocks) removed — now live in agent_task table
- Add `get_task_keys()` — returns list of all task keys for UI rendering
- Add `stringify_response_schema(task_key)` — returns formatted string for `{$RESPONSE_SCHEMA}` token
- Add `TOKEN_SOURCES` mapping — config-driven token-to-data-path definitions:
  - Each token maps to a source type and resolution path
  - Source types: `candidate` (dot-path into candidate dict), `config` (named resolver function), `company` (future — dot-path into company_data, designed for but not yet wired)
  - Adding new tokens is a config change, not a code change
- Update all references to `AGENT_CONFIG` across codebase (anthropic.py, consult.py, etc.)

**Layer:** src/utils/config.py, all files importing AGENT_CONFIG

- Database functions: `save_agent_task(task_key, ...)`, `get_agent_task(task_key)`, `list_agent_tasks()`

**Layer:** src/data/database.py

Title: anthropic.py DB-first prompt assembly and generic token resolver
Description:
**Scope:** Refactor do_task to fetch prompt content from DB and resolve merge tokens generically via TOKEN_SOURCES.

- `do_task` fetches agent_task record by task_key from database
- Resolves agent_id → fetches system prompt content from agent table
- Generic token resolution: regex finds all `{$TOKEN}` patterns in prompt text, looks each up in `TOKEN_SOURCES` from config, resolves value from the mapped source:
  - `candidate` source: dot-path walk into ctx (the candidate dict on the raft)
  - `config` source: calls named resolver function (e.g. `stringify_response_schema`)
  - `company` source: dot-path walk into company_data (designed for, not yet wired)
  - Unresolved tokens left as-is (no crash on unknown tokens)
- Assembles prompt identically to current shape — same output, different source
- Falls back to existing file-based assembly if no agent_task record found (migration safety)
- consult.py requires no changes — do_task interface unchanged

**Layer:** src/external/anthropic.py

Title: Candidate-as-raft: ctx threading through the call stack
Description:
**Scope:** Thread the candidate record dict (ctx / the raft) through the entire call stack so every layer has access to candidate data and API key.

- The candidate record dict from `get_candidate()` IS the context object (ctx) — no new dataclass
- `candidate_api_key` is already decrypted on the ctx by the time it's used — no separate `get_api_key()` function
- Add `ctx` parameter to consult.py: `render_verdict`, `qualify_job_listings`, `evaluate_jd` — all accept ctx and forward to `do_task`
- Add `ctx` parameter to roster.py: `prefilter_company` — accepts ctx and forwards to `do_task`
- consult.py and roster.py do not inspect or use ctx beyond forwarding
- `do_task` in anthropic.py accepts `ctx`:
  - When task `requires_candidate_key=True` (per CONFIG_TASKS): uses `ctx['candidate_api_key']` directly
  - `ctx['candidate_data']` provides all candidate content for TOKEN_SOURCES resolution

**Layer:** src/core/consult.py, src/core/roster.py, src/external/anthropic.py

Title: Candidate-scoped batch claims
Description:
**Scope:** Make candidate_id a required parameter on all batch claim functions so every run is single-candidate scoped.

- `claim_job_batch()` and `claim_company_batch()` in src/data/database.py REQUIRE `candidate_id` (not optional)
- All batch queries include `WHERE candidate_id = ?`
- `tracker.py` `get_new_job_batch()` and `roster.py` `get_new_company_batch()` require and pass `candidate_id` from ctx
- batch_id flows from dispatch_task record → CLI --batch-id argument → claim_*_batch
- Every run is single-candidate scoped — separate dispatch_task rows per candidate

**Layer:** src/data/database.py, src/core/tracker.py, src/core/roster.py

Title: dispatch_task table and database functions
Description:
**Scope:** Create dispatch_task table for scheduling configuration and dispatch_ledger table for run history.

- CREATE TABLE dispatch_task: `id` INTEGER PK, `candidate_id` TEXT FK, `task_key` TEXT, `last_run_at` TIMESTAMP (nullable), `freq_hrs` FLOAT, `min_count` INTEGER, `batch_id` TEXT (nullable — set when claimed by Dispatcher, cleared on completion), `enabled` BOOL, `updated_at` TIMESTAMP
- CREATE TABLE dispatch_ledger: `batch_id` TEXT PK, `task_key` TEXT, `candidate_id` TEXT FK, `started_at` TIMESTAMP, `completed_at` TIMESTAMP (nullable), `status` TEXT (RUNNING, COMPLETED, FAILED), `total_processed` INTEGER, `total_passed` INTEGER, `total_failed` INTEGER, `total_errors` INTEGER, `better_stack_url` TEXT
- Database functions for dispatch_task: `save_dispatch_task()`, `get_dispatch_task()`, `list_dispatch_tasks()`, `get_due_tasks()` (WHERE now > last_run_at + freq_hrs AND batch_id IS NULL AND enabled), `claim_dispatch_task(id, batch_id)`, `release_dispatch_task(id)`
- Database functions for dispatch_ledger: `save_dispatch_ledger()`, `update_dispatch_ledger()`, `get_dispatch_ledger(batch_id)`, `list_dispatch_ledger()` with filters
- Example dispatch_task rows: `freq_hrs=24, min_count=1` (daily floor), `freq_hrs=6, min_count=90` (high-volume trigger)
- Update database.py module docstring per ASTRAL_CODE_RULES 1.1

**Layer:** src/data/database.py

Title: Dispatcher orchestration logic
Description:
**Scope:** Build the dispatcher orchestrator that drives all scheduled batch processing. Lives in src/cli (orchestrator, not business logic).

- New file src/cli/dispatcher.py
- `get_due_tasks()`: query dispatch_task for due records, claim by setting batch_id (prevents cron double-dispatch), return claimed records
- `dispatch()` — main entry point called by cron endpoint:
  1. Call `get_due_tasks()`
  2. For each claimed record:
     a. Get `trigger_state` from CONFIG_TASKS for this task_key
     b. Query count of records in trigger_state for this candidate_id
     c. If count < `min_count`: skip, release dispatch_task, continue
     d. Fetch candidate record (the raft) via `candidate.get_candidate(candidate_id)`
     e. Validate `ctx['candidate_api_key']` — skip with error log if missing
     f. Write dispatch_ledger record (status=RUNNING)
     g. Invoke batch function directly (not subprocess) passing ctx + batch_id
     h. Await summary response
     i. Update dispatch_ledger (status=COMPLETED/FAILED, counts, completed_at, better_stack_url)
     j. Release dispatch_task: clear batch_id, set last_run_at=now()

**Layer:** src/cli/dispatcher.py

- POST /api/admin/dispatch_tasks — create new row (select candidate, task_key, freq_hrs, min_count)
- PUT /api/admin/dispatch_tasks/:id — update freq_hrs, min_count, enabled
- POST /api/admin/dispatch_tasks/:id/trigger — manual trigger (dispatch immediately regardless of threshold)
